gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Empty-day reward rollup: first staker after N inactive days claims N days of accumulated TORUS base emissions for 1 day of staking cost
candidate.claimed_root_cause: _checkAndUpdateRewardPool() rolls rewardPool[d] into penaltiesInRewardPool[currentDay] for every past day where totalShares[d]==0, with no eligibility check tying the recipient to those days and without zeroing rewardPool[d]
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker stakes 1 day after N inactive days and receives rewardPool[N+1] + sum(rewardPool[2..N]) via calculateStakingRewards, extracting ~N× their fair single-day entitlement as freshly minted TORUS

live_preconditions.name: totalShares[d] == 0 for at least one past day d since protocol start
live_preconditions.observed_value: not directly enumerated in live_context; protocol started at timestamp 1752170400 (~July 10 2025, ~369 days ago); totalShares mapping keys not captured; early-stage or intermittent inactivity is realistic
live_preconditions.source: live_context field: startTimeStamp=1752170400; totalShares mapping absent from mapping_key_hints

live_preconditions.name: rewardPool[1] == 100_000e18 (INITIAL_DAILY_REWARD set in constructor)
live_preconditions.observed_value: confirmed by source: constructor line 142 sets rewardPool[1] = INITIAL_DAILY_REWARD; BuyAndProcessConst.sol line 46 defines INITIAL_DAILY_REWARD = 100_000e18

live_preconditions.name: Attacker can acquire shares on currentDay via createTorus or stakeTorus (unprivileged)
live_preconditions.observed_value: createTorus and stakeTorus are public non-privileged functions; no role or whitelist check observed in source

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: For each past day d where totalShares[d]==0 and d!=currentDay, nextPool (==rewardPool[d]) is added to penaltiesInRewardPool[currentDay] at line 707; rewardPool[d] is set at line 704 but never zeroed, and no check ties the current staker to those days

Distributes both rewardPool[day] and penaltiesInRewardPool[day] pro-rata to whoever holds shares on that day with no distinction between penalty-origin and rolled-up-base-reward-origin funds; attacker holding 100% of totalShares[N+1] receives 100% of penaltiesInRewardPool[N+1]
Calls _checkAndUpdateRewardPool() then calculateStakingRewards() then torus.mint(msg.sender, totalAmount) with no eligibility check on the origin of penaltiesInRewardPool
Only rewardPool[1] is initialized; all subsequent days are computed lazily by _checkAndUpdateRewardPool, enabling the bulk rollup on first interaction after N inactive days
DAILY_REDUCTION_RATE=8 BPS and INITIAL_DAILY_REWARD=100_000e18 set the magnitude: for N=30 inactive days, penaltiesInRewardPool[31] accumulates ~29 x ~99,920e18 ≈ 2,897,680 TORUS

attacker_path.actor: any unprivileged EOA with sufficient TitanX or ETH to pay the createTorus fee
attacker_inputs: lengthInDays = 1 (minimum stake duration, minimizes cost while capturing full day-N+1 penaltiesInRewardPool)
call_sequence: 1. Wait N days with zero createTorus/stakeTorus/claim interactions so totalShares[d]==0 for d in [2..N]

2. Call createTorus(1, 1) on day N+1: _checkAndUpdateRewardPool() runs, loop d=2..N+1 sets rewardPool[d]=nextPool; for d in [2..N] where totalShares[d]==0, penaltiesInRewardPool[N+1] += nextPool; attacker becomes sole holder of totalShares[N+1]
4. Call claim(0): calculateStakingRewards returns rewardPool[N+1] + penaltiesInRewardPool[N+1]; torus.mint(attacker, principal + rewardPool[N+1] + penaltiesInRewardPool[N+1])
state_after: for N=30: attacker receives ~30x single-day reward entitlement (~2,997,600 TORUS vs fair ~99,920 TORUS)

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: TORUS tokens freshly minted to attacker via torus.mint() in claim()
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: rewardPool[N+1] + sum(rewardPool[2..N]); for N=30: ~100_000e18 + ~29 x ~99_920e18 ≈ 2,997,680 TORUS
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol mints excess TORUS beyond attacker entitlement; all existing TORUS holders are diluted; base daily emissions from days 2..N that should remain unclaimable (no stakers present) are instead extracted by the first staker
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker staked only on day N+1 and is entitled only to rewardPool[N+1]; penaltiesInRewardPool[N+1] contains rolled-up base emissions from days 2..N during which the attacker held zero shares and provided zero staking service; no guard checks whether the claimant was present during those days

setup: Deploy mock Torus ERC20 with mint authority granted to TorusCreateAndStake
Deploy mock TitanX ERC20; fund attacker with sufficient TitanX for createTorus fee
vm.warp(block.timestamp + 30 days) to simulate 30 days of inactivity with no stakers
expected_assertions: assertGt(penaltiesAccumulated, 0, 'penaltiesInRewardPool[31] must be non-zero after rollup of 29 empty days')
assertApproxEqAbs(penaltiesAccumulated, sumRewardPool2to30, 1e15, 'rolled-up amount equals sum of empty-day base rewards')
assertGt(attackerBalance - attackerPrincipal, rewardDay31 * 20, 'attacker received >20x single-day reward entitlement')
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If penaltiesInRewardPool[31] == 0 after createTorus call, or if attackerBalance - principal <= rewardPool[31], the vulnerability is not triggered in this deployment configuration
